nodes.label: MATH 3A
nodes.title: Calculus I
nodes.description: Limits, derivatives, and integrals—foundation for probability, finance, and actuarial modeling.

nodes.label: MATH 3B
nodes.title: Calculus II
nodes.description: Integration techniques, sequences, and series; prerequisite for linear algebra and vector calculus.

nodes.label: CMPSC 8
nodes.title: Introduction to Python
nodes.description: Python programming for data analysis and computation—required preparation for actuarial coursework.

nodes.label: MATH 4A
nodes.title: Linear Algebra
nodes.description: Vector spaces, matrices, and linear transformations; core math for statistics and actuarial models.

nodes.label: CMPSC 9
nodes.title: Intermediate Python
nodes.description: Intermediate Python and software skills; recommended over CMPSC 16 for actuarial majors.

nodes.label: MATH 6A
nodes.title: Vector Calculus I
nodes.description: Multivariable calculus: partial derivatives, multiple integrals, and vector fields.

nodes.label: MATH 4B
nodes.title: Differential Equations
nodes.description: Ordinary differential equations and applications in science, economics, and risk modeling.

nodes.label: PSTAT 10
nodes.title: Probability and Statistics
nodes.description: Introductory probability and statistics; gateway to upper-division PSTAT actuarial sequence.

nodes.label: MATH 8
nodes.title: Transition to Proofs
nodes.description: Proof writing and discrete math; required for upper-division probability and mathematical statistics.

nodes.label: MATH 6B
nodes.title: Vector Calculus II
nodes.description: Line and surface integrals, Green's, Stokes', and divergence theorems.

nodes.label: PSTAT 120A
nodes.title: Probability
nodes.description: Rigorous probability theory: random variables, distributions, and limit theorems for actuarial exams.

nodes.label: PSTAT 120B
nodes.title: Statistics
nodes.description: Statistical inference: estimation, hypothesis testing, and confidence intervals.

nodes.label: PSTAT 171
nodes.title: Stochastic Calculus
nodes.description: Brownian motion, Itô calculus, and stochastic processes for quantitative finance and actuarial science.

nodes.label: PSTAT 160A
nodes.title: Mathematical Statistics
nodes.description: Theory of point estimation, sufficiency, and statistical decision theory.

nodes.label: PSTAT 126
nodes.title: Regression Analysis
nodes.description: Linear and multiple regression, model diagnostics, and applied statistical modeling.

nodes.label: PSTAT 170
nodes.title: Mathematical Finance
nodes.description: Option pricing, portfolio theory, and financial derivatives using stochastic methods.

nodes.label: PSTAT 174
nodes.title: Financial Mathematics
nodes.description: Interest theory, annuities, and actuarial mathematics aligned with SOA Exam FM topics.

nodes.label: PSTAT 172A
nodes.title: Actuarial Models I
nodes.description: Life contingencies, survival models, and life insurance mathematics (Exam LTAM prep).

nodes.label: PSTAT 172B
nodes.title: Actuarial Models II
nodes.description: Loss models, credibility, and short-term insurance actuarial methods.

nodes.label: PSTAT 173
nodes.title: Actuarial Statistics
nodes.description: Statistical methods for actuarial modeling and Exam STAM preparation.

nodes.label: Actuary
nodes.title: SOA / CAS Actuary
nodes.description: Pricing and reserving for insurance, pensions, and enterprise risk—SOA or CAS credential track.

nodes.label: Risk Analyst
nodes.title: Enterprise Risk & Insurance
nodes.description: Quantify and manage financial, operational, and insurance risk for firms and regulators.

nodes.label: Quant Finance
nodes.title: Quantitative Finance
nodes.description: Derivatives pricing, trading strategies, and risk analytics in banking and asset management.

nodes.label: BS/MS Pathway
nodes.title: Combined BS/MS + Industry Thesis
nodes.description: UCSB actuarial BS/MS program with accelerated graduate coursework and industry thesis option.
